title: Vapi vs Retell AI for Voice Agents
use_case: Managed voice AI pipelines and telephony integration
latency_comparison: Retell AI uses a custom, highly optimized voice-first streaming pipeline that often shows slightly lower latency under high concurrency. Vapi relies on standard WebRTC endpoints and is highly customisable.
customizability: Vapi is a modular developer toolbox, letting you easily bring your own LLM, transcriber, and TTS provider. Retell AI is more of an integrated appliance, providing a smoother initial out-of-the-box experience.
crossover_math: At low volumes (<5,000 minutes/month), Vapi's lower platform fees are highly cost-effective. At high scale (>50,000 minutes/month), Retell AI's volume pricing is competitive, but building custom open-source pipelines becomes the logical crossover choice.
developer_experience: Vapi offers clean SDKs and direct control over WebSocket data feeds. Retell AI provides a cleaner drag-and-drop conversational designer for prompt layout.

title: Deepgram vs Soniox for Spanish Voice Transcription
use_case: Real-time speech-to-text (STT) for cross-border Spanish voice agents
latency_comparison: Deepgram's Nova-2 model features sub-300ms latency, making it ideal for conversational speed. Soniox features highly competitive transcription latency but specializes in deep phonetic precision.
spanish_accuracy: Soniox provides industry-leading accuracy for conversational Spanish, particularly in recognizing diverse regional Mexican dialects without getting tripped up by ambient noise. Deepgram Nova-2 is robust but can occasionally miss subtle phonetic transitions.
crossover_math: Deepgram's developer tier is excellent for multi-language bots. Soniox is the recommended choice for dedicated Spanish-first applications in regions like Mexico where dialectal precision dictates call conversion.
developer_experience: Deepgram has extensive SDK support and pre-built integrations with Vapi/Retell. Soniox requires custom API configurations for real-time WebSocket streams.

title: Claude Prompt Caching vs OpenAI for High-Volume Pipelines
use_case: Cost and speed optimization for agent systems with large persistent system prompts
latency_comparison: Claude prompt caching reduces time-to-first-token by up to 50% because the model skips parsing the cached prefix. OpenAI runs standard prompt evaluations unless using assistant thread caches.
customizability: Claude allows developers to set explicit breakpoints (up to 4 cache checkpoints) in their prompt. OpenAI manages caching automatically behind the scenes (less control).
crossover_math: For agents with large system prompts (>10k tokens) or long conversation histories, Claude prompt caching reduces input token cost by up to 90%. For short, stateless completions, OpenAI's raw token pricing remains highly competitive.
developer_experience: Anthropic Claude requires setting the `cache_control` header in the message payload. OpenAI requires no code modifications but offers no visibility into cache hit rates.
